name: Get User's Accessible Hubs
description: This endpoint returns a collection of accessible hubs for the authenticated user. Hubs represent BIM 360 Team hubs, Fusion Team hubs, A360 Personal hubs, and BIM 360 Docs accounts. Only active hubs are included in the response. The API also supports Autodesk Construction Cloud (ACC) projects.

headers.name: Authorization
headers.description: Bearer <token>, obtained via either a two-legged or three-legged OAuth flow.

headers.name: x-user-id
headers.description: In a two-legged authentication context, the app can act on behalf of the user specified in this header.

query_parameters.name: filter[id]
query_parameters.description: Filter hubs by their IDs.

query_parameters.name: filter[name]
query_parameters.description: Filter hubs by their name.

query_parameters.name: filter[extension.type]
query_parameters.description: Filter hubs by their extension type (team hubs, BIM 360 Docs accounts, personal hubs).

status_codes.200: OK — Successfully retrieved the collection of accessible hubs.
status_codes.401: Unauthorized — The Authorization header is invalid or missing.
status_codes.403: Forbidden — The request is valid but permission is denied.

name: Get Hub by ID
description: This endpoint returns detailed information about a specific hub identified by its hub ID. Hub IDs correspond to BIM 360 Docs account IDs (with a 'b.' prefix) and are compatible with Autodesk Construction Cloud (ACC) projects.

headers.name: Authorization
headers.description: Bearer <token>, obtained via either a two-legged or three-legged OAuth flow.

headers.name: x-user-id
headers.description: In a two-legged authentication context, limits access to the specified user.

uri_parameters.name: hub_id
uri_parameters.description: The unique identifier of the hub. Note that BIM 360 Docs uses the format 'b.<accountId>' for hub IDs.

status_codes.200: OK — Successfully retrieved the specific hub data.
status_codes.400: Bad Request — Malformed syntax or missing headers.
status_codes.403: Forbidden — Permission denied or application not white-listed.
status_codes.404: Not Found — The specified hub ID was not found.

name: Get Signed S3 Download URL
description: This endpoint generates a short-lived signed URL for direct S3 download, bypassing OSS servers. The signed URL typically expires in 2 minutes, though a custom expiration time between 1 and 60 minutes can be set. The signed URL allows downloading the object from S3 directly. If the object is being uploaded in chunks (resumable upload), the response will contain multiple S3 URLs, one per chunk. These can be used for parallelized downloads to maximize download speed.

uri_parameters.name: bucketKey
uri_parameters.description: The URL-encoded key of the bucket where the object is stored.

uri_parameters.name: objectKey
uri_parameters.description: The URL-encoded key of the object to generate a signed download URL for.

query_string_parameters.description: Specifies the expected Content-Type header for the response. Defaults to the object's content type.

query_string_parameters.name: response-content-disposition
query_string_parameters.description: Specifies the Content-Disposition header value for the response. Defaults to the object's content disposition.

query_string_parameters.name: response-cache-control
query_string_parameters.description: Specifies the Cache-Control header value for the response. Defaults to the object's cache control.

query_string_parameters.name: public-resource-fallback
query_string_parameters.description: Falls back to an OSS signed URL in case of unmerged resumable uploads.

query_string_parameters.name: useCdn
query_string_parameters.description: Generates a CloudFront URL for the S3 object.

query_string_parameters.name: minutesExpiration
query_string_parameters.description: Custom expiration time for the signed URL, between 1 and 60 minutes. Defaults to 2 minutes.

status_codes.400: BAD REQUEST — The request could not be understood due to malformed syntax or missing parameters.

status_codes.403: FORBIDDEN — Permission denied for the requested operation.

name: Get Batch Signed S3 Download URLs
description: This endpoint allows you to generate multiple short-lived signed URLs for downloading objects directly from S3, bypassing OSS servers. Each URL typically expires after 2 minutes, but you can set a custom expiration time of up to 60 minutes. The response can include multiple URLs, one for each requested object, with support for parallelized downloads using range-based URLs for large objects. If objects are uploaded in chunks, the response will include multiple signed URLs corresponding to each byte range chunk.

query_string_parameters.name: minutesExpiration

name: Get S3 Signed Upload URLs
description: This endpoint allows you to generate signed URLs for uploading objects to S3. For single-part uploads, a single signed URL is returned, while for multipart uploads, an array of signed URLs is provided, each corresponding to a specific chunk. The signed URLs typically expire after 2 minutes by default, but custom expiration times (up to 60 minutes) can be set. If a multipart upload is requested, the same uploadKey must be used to associate the new chunks with the original upload session. The endpoint also supports accelerated uploads with S3 acceleration, as well as custom expiration times.

query_string_parameters.name: minutesExpiration

uploadKey.description: The upload session identifier for the attempted completion.

expected.description: The expected object size provided in the request.

detected.description: The actual size of the object detected in S3.

part.description: The part index in the multipart upload.

status.description: The status of the part. Possible values are: 'Pending', 'Unexpected', 'TooSmall', 'Unexpected+TooSmall', or 'Ok'.

size.description: The size of the corresponding part in bytes.

eTag.description: The eTag of the part uploaded to S3.

name: Complete Multiple S3 Uploads
description: This endpoint allows users to complete the upload process for multiple objects after the bytes have been uploaded directly to S3. It accepts up to 25 objects per batch. An object will not be accessible until this completion process is triggered. The upload session must be completed either by this endpoint or the single Complete Upload endpoint. It also ensures the uploaded data matches the expected size and eTags for validation.

uri_parameters.name: bucketKey
uri_parameters.description: The URL-encoded name/key of the bucket containing the objects for which to complete the upload.

request_headers.name: Authorization
request_headers.description: Authorization header with Bearer <token>, where <token> is obtained via POST token.

requests.description: An array of objects representing each upload to complete.

objectKey.description: The key/name of the object for which to complete the upload.

uploadKey.description: The identifier of the upload session provided by OSS during the Get Upload URL/s request.

size.description: The expected size of the uploaded object for validation.

eTags.description: An array of eTags corresponding to the uploaded chunks or the full object. It is used to verify the integrity of the uploaded data.

x-ads-meta-Content-Type.description: The Content-Type value to store in the record for the uploaded object.

x-ads-meta-Content-Disposition.description: The Content-Disposition value to store in the record for the uploaded object.

x-ads-meta-Content-Encoding.description: The Content-Encoding value to store in the record for the uploaded object.

x-ads-meta-Cache-Control.description: The Cache-Control value to store in the record for the uploaded object.

status_codes.200: OK — The upload completion process has succeeded for all objects in the batch.
status_codes.400: BAD REQUEST — The request could not be understood due to malformed syntax or missing request headers.
status_codes.401: UNAUTHORIZED — The Authorization header was invalid or the token scope was not sufficient.
status_codes.403: FORBIDDEN — Permission denied for the requested operation.
status_codes.500: INTERNAL SERVER ERROR — An internal error occurred while processing the request.

results.description: A map of the returned results, where each key corresponds to an object key in the batch, and the value contains the results for that object.

status.description: Indicates whether the object completion failed. If absent, assume success. Possible value 'error' indicates a failure.

bucketKey.description: The key of the bucket into which the object was uploaded.

expected.description: The expected size of the object provided in the request.

detected.description: The actual size of the object detected in S3.

contentType.description: The Content-Type stored for the object.

contentDisposition.description: The Content-Disposition stored for the object.

contentEncoding.description: The Content-Encoding stored for the object.

cacheControl.description: The Cache-Control stored for the object.

part.description: The part index in the multipart upload.

status.description: The status of the part. Possible values are: 'Pending', 'Unexpected', 'TooSmall', 'Unexpected+TooSmall', or 'Ok'.

size.description: The size of the part detected in S3.

eTag.description: The eTag of the part detected in S3.

reason.description: The reason for failure if the status is 'error'.

name: Request Batch Signed S3 Upload URLs
description: This endpoint requests a batch of signed URLs for uploading multiple objects or chunks of objects to S3. The signed URLs expire after 2 minutes by default, but expiration time can be customized (1 to 60 minutes). The response may contain multiple URLs for multipart uploads, and these URLs can be used to upload data to S3. The request accepts up to 25 URLs per batch, and you must provide the same uploadKey for multipart uploads to associate new chunks with the original upload session.

uri_parameters.name: bucketKey
uri_parameters.description: The URL-encoded name/key of the bucket containing the objects for which to generate signed upload URLs.

query_string_parameters.name: useAcceleration
query_string_parameters.description: Specifies whether to generate accelerated signed URLs (s3-accelerate.amazonaws.com). Defaults to true. If set to false, the URLs will be standard S3 URLs.

query_string_parameters.name: minutesExpiration
query_string_parameters.description: The expiration time in minutes for the signed URLs. Must be between 1 and 60 minutes. Default is 2 minutes.

request_headers.name: Authorization
request_headers.description: Authorization header with Bearer <token>, where <token> is obtained via POST token.

requests.description: An array of objects representing each upload for which to generate signed URLs.

objectKey.description: The key/name of the object for which to generate an S3 upload URL.

firstPart.description: The index of an individual part for which to retrieve a chunk upload URL. This is used for multipart uploads.

parts.description: The number of chunk URLs to return for multipart uploads. If not provided, only one URL for the entire object will be returned.

uploadKey.description: The identifier of the previously-initiated upload, required when requesting chunk URLs for a multipart upload.

status_codes.200: OK — Signed URLs for the upload(s) have been successfully generated.
status_codes.400: BAD REQUEST — The request was malformed or missing required headers/parameters.
status_codes.401: UNAUTHORIZED — Invalid Authorization or insufficient token scope.
status_codes.403: FORBIDDEN — Permission denied to access the specified bucket.
status_codes.404: NOT FOUND — The specified bucket does not exist.
status_codes.429: RATE-LIMIT EXCEEDED — Too many API requests in a short time.
status_codes.500: INTERNAL SERVER ERROR — An error occurred while processing the request.

results.description: A map of the returned results, where each key corresponds to an object key in the batch, and the value contains the results for that object.

uploadKey.description: The identifier of the upload session, used to track and re-request chunk URLs.

uploadExpiration.description: The expiration timestamp for the upload. All parts must be uploaded before this timestamp, and the complete upload endpoint must be called.

urlExpiration.description: The expiration timestamp for the generated signed URLs. This is an approximation, as multiple parts may have different expiration times.

urls.description: An array of signed URLs. For a single-part upload, only one URL will be included. For multipart uploads, there will be one URL per part.

status.description: The status of the URL request. If this is 'error', it indicates that an issue occurred while generating the URL.

reason.description: The reason for failure, if the status is 'error'.
